pi_items.source: Task description requirement #1: 'Set the default font size to Large (at least 20px)'. Verified against verifier.py line 55 which checks: actual_default_font >= 20
Task description requirement #2: 'Set the minimum font size to 16px'. Verified against verifier.py line 63 which checks: actual_min_font >= 16

Task description requirement #3: 'Configure the homepage to https://www.usa.gov'. USA.gov is the official U.S. federal government information portal per https://www.usa.gov. Verified against verifier.py line 71 which checks: 'usa.gov' in homepage

Task description requirement #5: 'Verify your settings by visiting https://www.usa.gov and https://www.ssa.gov'. Both are real U.S. government websites: usa.gov is the federal information portal (https://www.usa.gov), ssa.gov is the Social Security Administration (https://www.ssa.gov/). Verified against verifier.py lines 86-99 which check for both sites in browser history

Task description requirement #6: 'Create a reference document at /home/ga/Desktop/accessibility_config_guide.txt listing the changes you made (font size, homepage URL) for other staff members'. Exact match with metadata value and export_result.sh line 30

privileged_info_summary: This is a Microsoft Edge accessibility configuration task requiring an agent to configure browser settings for elderly and low-vision users. The task mandates: setting default font size to at least 20 pixels, setting minimum font size to 16 pixels, configuring the homepage to usa.gov (the official U.S. federal government information portal), ensuring the home button is visible, and enabling the browser to open the homepage on startup. The agent must verify settings by visiting two real U.S. government websites: usa.gov and ssa.gov. Finally, the agent must create a documentation file at /home/ga/Desktop/accessibility_config_guide.txt containing information about the font size and homepage URL changes made. The verification system uses browser preference settings stored in the Chromium-based Edge configuration directory and browser history to confirm all requirements are met. The task uses real, publicly accessible government websites for verification purposes.
